No Poverty.reason: The course covers real estate finance and property, which are mechanisms related to Target 1.4 (access to property and financial services), but lacks a focus on the poor.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and explicitly includes learning about "sustainability considerations," which aligns with education for sustainable development (Target 4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: The course on property ownership and financial services relates to the mechanisms in Target 5.a, but does not explicitly address gender equality.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course focuses on "Real estate financial system" and "Real estate financial products," directly supporting Target 8.10 on strengthening domestic financial institutions and access to financial services.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course on financing "real estate development" is fundamental to developing infrastructure (Target 9.1), a key component of industry and innovation.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course covers the "Real estate financial system" and "risk analysis," which tangentially relates to Target 10.5 on regulating financial markets, but lacks a focus on inequality.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course on "Real Estate Investment" and "real estate development," combined with "sustainability considerations," directly addresses housing and sustainable urbanization (Targets 11.1, 11.3).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The core competency to "understand... sustainability considerations of business situations" directly aligns with encouraging sustainable corporate practices (Target 12.6) in the real estate sector.
evidence: understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations of business situations.

Climate Action.reason: The course's focus on "risk analysis" and "sustainability considerations" can be inferred to include climate-related risks to real estate, relevant to strengthening resilience (Target 13.1).

Life on Land.reason: The study of "real estate development" and "sustainability considerations" implicitly relates to land use planning and integrating ecosystem values into development processes (Target 15.9).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly covers "Real estate law and taxation" (不動產法制與稅賦), directly supporting the promotion of the rule of law (Target 16.3) and transparent institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's analysis of financial systems and risk contributes to understanding macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13), and its inclusion of "sustainability considerations" supports policy coherence (Target 17.14).
